summary: Frontend-only E2E test of the Spotify-Wrapped-style Dia dos Namorados experience at mobile viewport 390x844. All 10 requested scenarios passed: start slide CTA, auto-advance to stats (~7.5s), placeholder [INSIRA AQUI] stats with correct labels, auto-advance to music quiz, wrong-answer shake-and-red without advancing, correct-answer confetti + auto-advance to photos, 4 polaroids, final love message with [INSIRA AQUI], Te amo button spawns 36 heart-rain DOM nodes in #heart-rain-container, stories progress bar with 4 segments visible, no horizontal overflow on mobile, zero console errors.

issues: Counters with [INSIRA AQUI] placeholder render but appear visually faint/empty in screenshot due to delayed staggered fade-in (delays 0.35s/0.65s/0.95s) combined with flex justify-between leaving large empty gap between heading and counters. When real numbers are inserted the layout will be fine, but with the literal placeholder string the screen looks empty for ~1s after entering. Consider reducing the fade-in delays or top padding so content fills the viewport faster on mobile.

CTA button text 'COMEÇAR A RETROSPECTIVA' wraps to two lines inside the rounded pill on a 390px viewport. Still tappable, but visual rhythm slightly off — consider shrinking horizontal padding or letter-spacing at <400px.

action_items: (Optional polish) Reduce stagger delay on StatsSlide counters or restructure justify-between so the slide doesn't look empty on mobile while waiting for the 7.5s auto-advance.
(Optional polish) Tighten StartSlide CTA padding/tracking at <400px width so 'Começar a Retrospectiva' stays on one line.
critical_code_review_comments: WrappedExperience.jsx handleScreenTap: edge taps advance on slides 1 (stats) and 3 (photos) only — slide 3 is the final slide and `advance()` is capped via Math.min, so right-edge taps on photos are effectively no-ops. This matches the spec ('only on slides 1 and 3') but worth confirming intent: right-edge tap on the photos slide does nothing visible, which may confuse users.

WrappedExperience.jsx auto-advance useEffect depends on `progressKey` but progressKey is only bumped by advance()/goBack(); this is fine but the dependency is a noop in practice — minor cleanup possible.

StoriesProgress.jsx: when `active` is false on the current segment with a finite duration, animate width collapses to '0%' with a 0.6s ease — visually OK but inconsistent with 'past' segments being fully white. Acceptable.
MusicQuizSlide.jsx: lockedRef prevents repeated correct clicks but disabled={correctIdx !== null} already guards via React — defensive duplication is fine.

PhotosSlide.jsx spawnHearts directly manipulates DOM (document.getElementById) — works because the container is rendered in same component, but escapes React. Acceptable for this animation-only use case.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 10 requested flows pass at 390x844. Tested URL: https://love-wrapped-2.preview.emergentagent.com. Key data-testids used: wrapped-experience, start-wrapped-button, start-headline, stats-slide, stat-item-{days,pizzas,calls}, music-quiz-slide, quiz-option-{0,1,2}, photos-slide, polaroid-photo, final-love-message, te-amo-button, stories-segment-{0..3}. Confetti renders a <canvas>; heart rain creates 36 .heart-rain spans inside #heart-rain-container. Quiz wrong answers add bg color class containing '9F1239' (rose-900-ish) and reset after 700ms. Correct answer auto-advances after 2400ms.
